_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave E (the social city: EM-113 typed relationships, EM-114 children, EM-120 factions+reputation, EM-125 reflection bonds, EM-184 world-scale miracles, EM-185 GRANT-a-petition + social-graph UI)

resolution.summary: Wave-level adversarial QE on build/wave-e-social-world (B1 fd8ae50, B2 90fd3a8, B3 5e2a2e6, B4 106d518, B5 bbf6e57, B6 bd780e8) per contracts/wave-e.md v1.1 §Gates. The mandate was cross-batch composition, wave-level free-scale, and the green-gate rule — all executed, with 5 permanent tests added in backend/tests/test_wave_e_qe.py. (1) COMPOSITION CHAIN proven through the real scheduler + TickLoop: mutual partners → a birth at the round-1 boundary → the newborn's engine-assigned family edges complete a 3-warm component → faction_formed rides the SAME boundary drain BEHIND [child_spawned, agent_spawned] (births-before-factions order, all turn_id null, tick 0), parents debited 6 each, child at background tier — and Ada's very next actually-assembled LLM prompt carries 'Your circle: Ada's circle (3 members)'; the background child consults the LLM zero times across rounds 1-3 and the stable round-2/3 boundaries park zero faction events (the diff rule at wave level). (2) MIRACLE→SOCIETY CHAIN: calm_spirits on a pair at trust 28/interactions 5 flips them to friends INSIDE the miracle's own event batch ([god_miracle, relationship_changed ×2], trust 31, B1 reflex via _update_trust, outbox fully drained), an ally pair at interactions 1 is nudged (33) but NOT flipped, every living mood lifts including the remote background agent, and at the next round boundary the new warm edge completes the 3-member faction (the edgeless remote agent stays outside); the miracle emission adds ZERO llm_call rows. The salience half is proven at the runtime seam (push_event: global witness, importance 2.0, witnessed_importance is the EXACT trigger set, and the salient due turn really consults the LLM once) — which surfaced THE wave finding: the production /api/god/intervene handler never calls runtime.push_event, verified BY EXECUTION (TestClient cast → god_miracle persisted, but zero witnesses, empty importance accumulator, empty witnessed_since_llm), so the contracted 'whole town can react' belief-loop closure is unreachable from the god console / GRANT button today (MAJOR, one-line fix; same pre-existing gap shape as API-injected random_event). (3) WAVE-LEVEL FREE-SCALE at the contracted seam: a world with every wave-E block at its shipped enabled:true default but no partners and no miracles, driven 30 full rounds through next_agent()+_execute_turn, produces EXACTLY the pre-E cadence-math llm_call count — 71 rows (2 protagonists × 30 + supporting × 10 + background first_turn × 1, reflex at rounds 20/30) over 73 turns, with the router call log agreeing at 71, zero births, zero factions, zero rows of any of the 8 new kinds; the derivation is documented in the test. (4) FORK FIDELITY: a world carrying ALL wave-E state at once (partner edges with since_tick 7, a born child with parents + family ties stamped tick 12, a live faction, an active send_rain miracle until 55) round-trips to_snapshot→from_snapshot→to_snapshot json-byte-equal (EM-155), and the restored world BEHAVES: rain still buffs forage (+2), are_partners holds, recompute_factions is a zero-event no-op with the same faction id, the DERIVED pair cooldown blocks a re-birth at tick 611 and re-opens at exactly 612 (proving the gate reads restored state), and the rain expires exactly once on schedule with the buff gone. (5) GREEN-GATE AUDIT: git diff 9b98b24..bd780e8 shows ZERO pre-existing test files modified (only 5 new files — no assertion could have been weakened); the EM-161 protagonist fixture guard still reads the committed fixture byte-for-byte (read_text + equality; the _capture_fixture regenerator is a non-test helper; fixture file untouched all wave); B2's at-cap test holds population AT cap (2 living, max_population=2) and over it (1), across 120 ticks with every other gate ripe; B5's bless regression asserts the exact payload {kind, amount, before: 40.0, after: 70.0} and response JSON, not status alone; B4's zero-delta test compares actual llm_call ROW lists between bonded and plain reflection turns; B3's stability test drives 20 real boundaries. Full backend suite 703/703 (566 pre-wave baseline + 132 batch + 5 QE); frontend vitest 564/564 and npm run build green; wave-E feed registry entries are CSS var() tokens (no new hex in JS).

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-e.md v1.1 clause-by-clause through the batch suites (132 tests) plus the QE composition probes. B1: since_tick additive round-trip (snapshot + repository json, absent⇒0), the single _update_trust reflex seam (friend at ≥30/≥5, feud at ≤-40, no auto-downgrade, steal escalation preserved beneath), partner trust-gate with guidance, family engine-only, are_partners mutual consent, relationship_changed on type edges only with agent-id endpoints, EM-161 fixture byte-identical. B2: all gates (population cap AT cap, beds, credits both-pay sink, energy, derived pair cooldown, sha1-seeded chance, one-birth-per-round), background tier, persona casting incl. dead-name skip and Kit-N, load-spread profile, enabled:false byte-identical. B3: derived reputation (documented seam deviation: computed in to_snapshot, contract-allowed), mutual-warm components, ≥50%-overlap identity continuity, diff-driven events, deterministic sha1 ids/names, enabled:false byte-identical; item 6's prompt line correctly deferred to and wired by B4 (faction_of). B4: bond rides the same reflection call (llm_call row-list equality), B1 guards enforced, silent drops with trace reasons, bond_applied observability, non-reflection prompt byte-identical. B5: forage/decay MATH verified (not flags), refresh-not-stack, calm one-time with B1 transitions firing, the v1.1 ValueError→422 erratum honored, targeted bless/grant payload-byte-identical, snapshot mid-rain resume + on-schedule expiry. B6: all 8 kinds in all three registries (enumerated test), GRANT on agent petitions only, both API calls with right payloads, EM-141 filter preserved, reputation absent-safe, tokens-not-hex. DOCKED ONE POINT for the B5 item-3 production gap: the contract's stated purpose — god_miracle 'globally witnessed... so the whole town can react — this closes the ask→answer→belief loop' — is met at the runtime constants (weight 2.0 + global witness kind, both tested) but is UNREACHABLE from the only production cast path: /api/god/intervene emits to DB/WS and never calls runtime.push_event (execution-verified: zero witnesses after a real API cast). World-state effects (mood, forage, decay, trust) DO land; the memory/salience/reflection half of the loop does not.

test_coverage.notes: 132 new batch tests (23 B1 + 31 B2 + 24 B3 + 27 B4 + 27 B5) + 5 permanent QE tests (backend/tests/test_wave_e_qe.py) in a 703-green backend suite, plus 71 new frontend tests in a 564-green vitest suite. Assertion quality is high across the wave: math not flags (forage 1+2, decay×0.5 measured), repo row-lists not counts, exact payload pins, real next_agent()/TickLoop drives, byte-identical disabled paths proven per batch, EMBEDDED_WORLD_YAML mirrors pinned. QE adds the four cross-batch shapes no per-batch lens could see: the birth→faction→prompt composition through one real loop, the miracle→friend-flip→faction chain with the salience seam isolated to exactly ['witnessed_importance'], the 30-round wave-default free-scale count (71 llm_calls, derivation documented), and the all-state-at-once fork byte-equality with behavioral resume incl. the cooldown re-opening at exactly tick 612. Honest gaps: (1) no test exercises the API→runtime witnessing path — because it does not exist (the MAJOR finding); the QE salience test pins the runtime semantics the fix must land on; (2) the GRANT flow is mocked-client only (B6 contract-conformant; no e2e browser pass this wave); (3) child agent ids are uuid4 so no test can pin cross-run birth reproducibility (issue 2).

security.notes: New API surface is the widened POST /api/god/intervene body: agent_id str|None with a strict matrix (world kind + agent_id ⇒ 422, targeted kind without ⇒ 422, both engine-seam ValueErrors), amount pydantic-validated 1..100, unknown/dead targets rejected (no resurrection reach), miracles disabled ⇒ 422 with targeted kinds untouched — all proven by the B5 API tests against the real app. No injection surface: miracle kinds are a closed allowlist, no user text reaches the engine through this endpoint. DoS-shape checked: active_miracles is refresh-not-stack (bounded list), births are double-capped (population ≤ 25 + home beds) with one-per-round, faction recompute is O(N²) at the 25-agent cap once per round, reputation is O(N) per agent at serialization — all bounded. XSS/reflection on the GRANT path checked: petition text is rendered through React text nodes only (zero dangerouslySetInnerHTML/innerHTML in web/src, verified by grep), the god billboard reply caps the quote at 160 chars + in_reply_to at 120, and the backend billboard cap (280) applies; wave-E feed colors are CSS var() tokens, no new hex-in-JS. One security-adjacent observation (not docked, no new trust boundary): the GRANT reply re-broadcasts agent-authored petition text under the god's voice, which then rides other agents' prompts via the god board-note channel — agent billboard text already reaches co-located prompts directly, so this amplifies an existing channel rather than opening one; the click is human-initiated. Free-scale law upheld wave-wide and execution-verified: zero standing LLM calls added (QE probe 3), bonds ride the existing reflection call (row-list equality), miracles/factions/reputation are pure state. Tests are MockProvider/scripted-fakes only, ':memory:' DBs, no network, no real keys.

regression_risk.notes: Everything is additive and config-gated with byte-identical disabled proofs per batch (snapshot key-set preservation for birth-free/faction-free/miracle-free worlds — the cap_demotions only-when-non-empty pattern throughout), and the full suites are green: backend 703/703 with the 566 pre-wave baseline intact, frontend 564/564 + build. The green-gate audit found ZERO pre-existing test files modified across the wave (git diff 9b98b24..bd780e8 touches only the 5 new test files) — no assertion was relocated or weakened. Hot paths touched and their guards: _update_trust now runs _maybe_shift_relationship on EVERY trust mutation (give/steal/insult/attack and calm_spirits — pure dict math, no calls; transitions never auto-downgrade so existing feud/enemy drama is preserved; the pre-E steal escalation is explicitly untouched beneath it); _apply_round_start gained check_births + recompute_factions (pointer-safe: QE's 30-round drive confirms the EM-158/172 rotation invariants hold with the hooks live, and a spawned background child never joins a round whose tier isn't due); apply_energy_decay/action_forage gained miracle_active checks (two list scans over an almost-always-empty list); to_snapshot now computes reputation per agent per broadcast (O(N²) at 25 agents ≈ 625 dict lookups per WS frame — negligible, and gated off with factions.enabled). Live-behavior watch items: (a) new relationship words (partner/feud/mentor/family) now appear in prompts when transitions fire — intended, but model outputs may shift on long-running worlds; (b) calm_spirits can cascade friend-flips on dense towns (clamped, diff-driven, at most one event per directed edge); (c) the transient outboxes (pending_spawn_events / pending_relationship_events) are not serialized — unreachable as a loss in the TickLoop (drained at the turn following every boundary, snapshots saved post-drain) but a fork-anywhere future should know.

frontend.command: cd web && npx vitest run && npm run build
frontend.result_line: Test Files 47 passed (47); Tests 564 passed (564); vite build ✓ built in 4.61s (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only)

test_results.qe_probes: Verified BY EXECUTION: (1) full backend suite 703 passed ('703 passed, 1 warning in 29.32s'; 698 collected with the QE file deselected — the wave's 566+132 arithmetic checks out); (2) frontend vitest 564 passed + npm run build green; (3) the MAJOR finding reproduced live: a TestClient POST /api/god/intervene {kind: send_rain} returns 200 with until_tick and persists the god_miracle row, while runtime._memory shows ZERO witnesses, runtime._importance is empty, and _witnessed_since_llm is empty — the importance-weight/global-witness machinery (which works when push_event IS called, proven in both the B5 test and QE's salience test) is never invoked by the API handler (grep: app.py contains no push_event/_runtime reference; the only production push_event call sites are loop.py:636 turn events, loop.py:773 animal events, run.py:124 CLI turn events); (4) all five QE tests pass against the shipped code and each failed appropriately during development when pointed at wrong expectations (e.g. the free-scale count is exact-equality 71, not >=). Verified BY READING: the five backend batch diffs end-to-end (no silenced assertions, no weakened fixtures — zero pre-existing test files touched, fixture file em161_protagonist_prompt_pre_diet.txt last modified in 5a23019, pre-wave); world.py's wave-E seams (the single reflex seam after the trust clamp; the outbox patterns incl. calm_spirits' self-drain because no action turn ever drains god mutations; check_births' derived cooldown via the child's family-tie since_tick; recompute_factions' greedy best-overlap continuity with sha1 ids; to_snapshot/from_snapshot additive keys); runtime.py's bond sanitize-then-apply path and the faction prompt line gated to empty for faction-free worlds (fixture guard safe); loop.py's reset clears (factions={}, active_miracles=[]) and the expiry sweep beside expire_blackouts; app.py's widened InterveneBody and batch emission; the B6 GRANT component (React-escaped petition text, capped quotes, token-var colors, no innerHTML).

issues.title: god_miracle is never witnessed by agents in production — /api/god/intervene emits the batch to DB/WS but never calls runtime.push_event, so the contracted ask→answer→belief loop does not close from the god console or the GRANT button
issues.detail: contracts/wave-e.md B5 item 3: 'Add god_miracle to the runtime importance weights at 2.0 AND to the global witness kinds (alongside random_event) so the whole town can react — this closes the ask→answer→belief loop.' Both constants shipped and behave exactly as contracted WHEN push_event is invoked (proven by test_wave_e_miracles.py::test_god_miracle_is_globally_witnessed and the new QE salience test: remote background agent gains the memory line, importance 2.0, witnessed_importance trigger, and a real salient LLM turn). But the only production cast path — POST /api/god/intervene (the god console MIRACLES row and the EM-185 GRANT flow both call it) — loops the event batch through _loop._emit_event only (app.py:1160-1173): persisted + broadcast, never pushed into agent memory. Execution-verified: after a real API cast, zero agents hold a god_miracle memory, the importance accumulator is empty, and no background agent becomes salient. Consequences: agents feel the world-state effects (rain forage, decay, moods, trust nudges — those mutate world state directly and DO work) but never 'see' the miracle: no memory line, no reflection-importance contribution, no background wake-up, so the town cannot react in words to an answered prayer — the wave's headline loop runs ask→answer and stops before belief. The per-batch lens could not catch this (its tests call push_event directly); the gap is exactly the cross-batch composition QE exists for. Note the same gap already existed for API-injected random_event (inject_random_event also never pushes — the global-witness list is only effective for turn-chain events like rule_passed, plus everything on CLI runs via run.py:124), so B5 wired god_miracle faithfully 'alongside random_event' — including alongside its bug. Cheap fix: in app.py's god_intervene (and ideally inject_random_event), after _emit_event, call _loop._runtime.push_event({**e, 'tick': _world.tick}) for the god_miracle (or add a loop-level emit_and_witness helper); the QE test pins the runtime semantics the fix lands on, and a one-assertion extension of test_api_send_rain_200... locks the wiring.

issues.title: Child agent ids are uuid4-derived, so identical worlds produce different children across runs — the only non-seeded randomness wave E ships
issues.detail: check_births' chance gate is contract-perfect (sha1 over city_seed:tick:pair, no random module), but the child is created through the pre-existing spawn_agent machinery (world.py:2908), whose id is f'agent_{name}_{uuid4[:6]}'. Two runs from identical seeds/state birth the same child at the same tick with the same name — but different agent ids, diverging event payloads and downstream sort orders (faction membership order, sorted-id walks). The EM-101 fork path is unaffected (from_snapshot restores serialized ids — QE's byte-equality proves it), and event-log replay folds events rather than re-simulating, so no shipped feature breaks. Recorded against the EM-155 determinism law ('no random without a seed derived from world state') because the contract's 'existing spawn_agent machinery' phrasing inherited the gap rather than blessing it. Cheap fix if cross-run reproducibility ever matters: derive the suffix from sha1(city_seed, tick, parents) in spawn_agent's birth path.

issues.title: Transient event outboxes (pending_spawn_events / pending_relationship_events) are not serialized — a snapshot taken between a round boundary and its drain would drop queued narrative events
issues.detail: Births/faction diffs park events at the round boundary (inside next_agent) and the loop drains them at the top of the NEXT _execute_turn; relationship transitions park and drain within the same turn. TickLoop's snapshot cadence saves AFTER the turn (post-drain), so the loss window is unreachable through the shipped loop — to_snapshot is only ever called with empty outboxes, and the QE fork test drains before snapshotting to mirror that. State is never lost (the child agent, faction dict, and relationship types are all serialized); only the one-shot narrative rows (child_spawned/faction_formed/relationship_changed) could vanish if a future caller snapshots mid-boundary (e.g. a fork-at-arbitrary-point feature). Filed as a known sharp edge for the EM-126 generations work that will build on the same outboxes; no action needed this wave.

issues.title: GRANT re-broadcasts agent-authored petition text in the god's voice — an amplified (not new) prompt-injection-shaped channel
issues.detail: The EM-185 GRANT reply posts '✦ Granted — … In answer to: "{petition}"' as a god billboard note (quote capped 160, in_reply_to 120, backend body cap 280). Rendering is safe (React text nodes only; zero dangerouslySetInnerHTML in web/src; CSS token colors), and the action is human-initiated. The observation: agent-generated text now rides OTHER agents' prompts through the god board-note channel, which carries more narrative authority than a peer's billboard post — an agent could craft a petition hoping a watcher GRANTs it verbatim. No new trust boundary is crossed (agent billboard text already reaches co-located prompts directly, and the god whisper/proclamation channels already inject arbitrary human text), so this is a note for the EM-186-era moderation thinking, not a defect.

gate_decision.reason: All hard gates are green and the wave's load-bearing claims survive adversarial verification by execution: backend 703/703 (566 pre-wave baseline intact + 132 batch + 5 QE), frontend 564/564 + build green; the contract-mandated gate probes all pass through real seams (a birth really debits BOTH parents and the child takes really-scheduled turns — background cadence proven by the QE turn ledger; a miracle really changes forage/decay MATH; the bond path emits row-identical llm_call lists; factions really diff — 20-stable-round and wave-level 30-round silence proofs); the wave-level free-scale law holds exactly (71 llm_calls == the pre-E cadence-math expectation with every wave-E block enabled); fork fidelity is json-byte-equal with behavior resuming on all four wave-E surfaces; and the green-gate audit found zero pre-existing tests touched, a byte-for-byte fixture guard, a genuinely-AT-cap cap test, and payload-equality regressions. ONE MAJOR finding (no CRITICAL): the ask→answer→belief loop's final leg — agents WITNESSING a god_miracle — is dead from the production API because app.py never pushes the batch into runtime memory (execution-verified); world-state effects land, so miracles are functional but mute. Per the wave's gate rules (proceed=false only on a CRITICAL blocker) the gate is GREEN, but the MAJOR must be fixed before BUILD_RESULTS closeout claims the loop is closed: it is a ~2-line app.py change landing on QE-pinned runtime semantics. The three MINORs (uuid4 child ids vs EM-155, unserialized outboxes, GRANT echo channel) go to plan-intake.
